summary: High‑energy 1984 Augusta show captured on a mostly soundboard source; stellar vocals and soaring Morning Dew offset by occasional audience patch glitches.
review: The performance is widely praised for its raw power – Jerry’s voice on the Dew, Phil’s booming bass, Brent’s rare “Don’t Need Love” moment and an unforgettable second‑set flow that builds from Stranger through a blazing Sailor > Saint into a soaring Uncle John’s Band → Jam → Morning Dew.  The tape is primarily a master soundboard cassette but contains an audience‑sourced patch for the latter part; reviewers note some left‑channel static, missing drums in the space segment and occasional hiss, yet most consider it a good‑to‑excellent listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: The overwhelming majority of reviews (4‑5 stars) celebrate the musical performance, especially the second set and Morning Dew, while technical complaints are limited to patch quality and minor noise – a classic 70 % show / 30 % recording weighting yields a rating in the strong‑good range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Primarily master soundboard cassette (MSC) with an audience (AUD) patch from mid‑UJB to end; reviewers note occasional left‑channel static, a hiss, missing drums/space in the patched segment and a brief dropout, but overall clarity is solid.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds momentum with a fiery Stranger and tight Cold Rain & Snow; second set erupts into a high‑energy marathon – Jack‑a‑Roe, Sailor > Saint, a soaring UJB → Jam → Morning Dew – delivering the “post‑drums” climax that many fans cite as the show’s defining moment.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s voice described as powerful, soulful and ‘like a mushroom cloud’; his guitar tone on the Dew is praised; occasional vocal strain noted but largely revered.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass bombs are highlighted as shaking rafters; sustained notes give depth to the mix.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s backing vocals and “Bobby licks” receive compliments, especially on rare electric version of On the Road Again.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (Leslie) gets credit for ‘Don’t Need Love’ riff and vocal support; keyboards noted as adding color throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming is praised early on but missing in the space segment due to the AUD patch; overall drum work considered tight.
